Assignment 1 - Shader Live Coding <br/>
Sarah Hatch <br/>
March 23rd 2026 <br/>

[Video of shader](https://vimeo.com/1176402534?share=copy&fl=sv&fe=ci) <br/>
[Shader itself](https://charlieroberts.codeberg.page/TheSchwartz/?)
[A1_ShaderCode.txt](A1_ShaderCode.txt)


Technically I wanted to test differnt effects to use with simple shapes. I stuck with circles for this assignment as I wanted to test what
I could do with the shape more than what shapes I could make. Sin and cos waves were especially helpful for making things feel like they
were pulsing. I looked at moving objects with those waves including changing the boundaries of an object and its position. I then looked at how for loops could be used to duplicate an image around the screen. This was a bit tricky for me to figure out how to use the output from each loop. I was able to get a line and grid of dots, but I struggled to modify their position further. One thing I tried but did not suceed on was getting the line of dots to wave, using a sin wave based on position led to some strange warped results. I know wonder if moving it based on loop iteration would have been more effective, something to try in the future. The last major technique I used was adding feedback from the previous frame. I used this both for the swirling circle effect and for the colored after shadows of elements near the end. I specifically tried to have a large offset on the position of the feedback to make it more apparent.


Visually I went with a constant pulse of sunset colors. This way the color was unifying and not totally boring but I could focus on the 
shapes. My piece reminds me of sunsets and space, which aren't particularliy related I suppose but thats the vibes I was working with.
I used lots of spreading and shrinking for the sunset lense, as well as the color. The lone light on a black background always
makes me think of space. I went with the approach of pretiming everything to move through a series of different shaders, grouping the effects by technique and visual similarities. I also tried to have a flow of that slowly built up to more complex as it went and then built back down. 

I recieved generally positive feedback on my piece. More than one person commented that it looked like a black hole, so I was succesful in my themeing. The biggest piece of feedback I got was that the section with the dots felt out of place, especially the part where they are stationary. I agree and if I had more time to work on this I would try to make them fit in better. I think figuring out transitions between the different effects would be something to pursue later. The dots could fit but they would need to be introduced less abruptly.
